title: Pin runtime dependencies with a lockfile, keep pyproject ranges loose
description: Declare compatible version ranges in pyproject.toml but resolve and commit an exact lockfile so builds are reproducible across machines.
body: Use when adding or upgrading a dependency in a packaged project. Do not pin exact == versions in pyproject [project.dependencies] (it blocks downstream resolution) and do not ship without a lock. Actions: declare ranges (e.g. ">=2,<3") in pyproject, run the resolver (uv lock / poetry lock / pip-compile) to produce a fully pinned lock with hashes, install from the lock in CI (uv sync --frozen / pip install --require-hashes), and verify the wheel imports in a clean venv. Validation signals include a byte-stable lockfile across two machines and `pip check` reporting no broken requirements. Risk boundary: --require-hashes failures are supply-chain signals, not noise; deny the install and review the changed hash before overriding.
